context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=3; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, or potatoes THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected substantial reheating for observed hot meals in laptop-working contexts. IF food is fruit or another cold-served item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal or social THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed cold fruit meal was explicitly preferred without reheating.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a front-facing focal activity such as watching TV, working on a laptop, or interacting with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred preserving the front view in front-focused contexts. No supported evidence yet for left- or right-specific occlusion preferences, including when a laptop is positioned to the left during work.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition such as fruit pieces or hot solid foods AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social or task-focused THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option across observed feeding meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social or task-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN yes BECAUSE the user consistently accepted explicit web confirmation of bite acquisition, indicating a stable preference for reliability despite extra interaction time and despite using assistive web access.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF food is being transferred for feeding AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward due to limited trunk control, and they consistently selected inside-mouth transfer across observed meals.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user consistently selected inside-mouth transfer and their physical profile supports that mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with another person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user preferred a nonverbal readiness cue in the observed social meal, consistent with reducing conversational disruption. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the newer laptop-working meal showed a preference for a quieter visual-only cue, outweighing the earlier multimodal laptop example and suggesting a stable tendency toward nonverbal signaling during focused work.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation in the observed social meal. IF feeding task AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred more automation when attention was occupied. This indicates a tie-break tendency toward automation under divided attention, while still avoiding button-based control due to physical limitations.

IF drinking task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for drinking in the observed social meal. IF drinking task AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for wiping in the observed social meal. IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with another person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user preferred a nonverbal cue when the tool reached transfer position in the observed social meal. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the newer laptop-working meal showed a preference for a quieter visual-only completion cue, outweighing the earlier multimodal laptop example and suggesting a stable tendency toward nonverbal signaling during focused work.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, which fits their inability to reliably press buttons.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social or task-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social or task-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in both social and laptop-working contexts.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred limited dipping in a social meal, consistent with reducing messiness. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid dipping in the observed laptop meal, suggesting a stronger mess-avoidance preference when occupied. IF food has no dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in the observed social meal, balancing efficiency with enough time to respond using assistive interaction methods. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a much longer wait when attention was divided, indicating a stable tie-break toward giving themselves ample intervention time during distraction.
